page: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a single thread per core configuration, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and a bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD for storage. Network connectivity is supported by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score both placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also a highlight, showing strong results in large-block read and write bandwidth tests, as well as a high PassMark memory score. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. The server demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing capabilities, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput perform at average levels.

From a resource density perspective, the b3-64 offers a balanced allocation of dedicated compute resources and memory without hyperthreading, ensuring predictable performance for workloads that do not benefit from virtual threads. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage provides a cost-efficient, self-contained solution for applications requiring fast local disk access without the overhead of external storage. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, text processing, web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: The OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose server configured with an AMD EPYC Milan processor on the x86_64 architecture. It delivers 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs at 2.3 GHz without hyperthreading, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, the server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU performance and robust large-block memory bandwidth, while multi-core compute and database operations perform in the average tier. This balanced hardware profile makes the b3-64 a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring predictable dedicated CPU resources, such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and moderate database applications.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-64 is a general-purpose server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated sixteen-core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory and local NVMe storage for predictable general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
64 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suitable for software compilation, text processing, and web serving
